en: And getting into a boat he crossed over and came to his own city.
zh: 耶穌上了船，過到自己的城來。

en: And behold, some people brought to him a paralytic, lying on a bed. And when Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, "Take heart, my son; your sins are forgiven."
zh: 有人把一個躺在床上的癱子帶到他那裡。耶穌看見他們的信心，就對癱子說：“孩子，放心！你的罪赦了。”

en: And behold, some of the scribes said to themselves, "This man is blaspheming."
zh: 有幾位經學家彼此說：“這個人在說僭妄的話。”

en: But Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said, "Why do you think evil in your hearts?
zh: 耶穌看出他們所想的，就說：“你們心裡為甚麼存著惡念呢？

en: For which is easier, to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Rise and walk'?
zh: 說‘你的罪赦了’，或說‘起來行走’，哪一樣容易呢？

en: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins"- he then said to the paralytic- "Rise, pick up your bed and go home."
zh: 然而為了要使你們知道人子在地上有赦罪的權柄， 起來，拿起你的床，回家去吧。”

en: And he rose and went home.
zh: 他就起來回家去了。

en: When the crowds saw it, they were afraid, and they glorified God, who had given such authority to men.
zh: 群眾看見，就起了敬畏的心，頌讚那把這樣的權柄賜給人的 神。

en: As Jesus passed on from there, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, "Follow me." And he rose and followed him.
zh: 耶穌從那裡往前走，看見一個人，名叫馬太，坐在稅關那裡，就對他說：“來跟從我！”他就起來跟從了耶穌。

en: And as Jesus reclined at table in the house, behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and were reclining with Jesus and his disciples.
zh: 耶穌在屋裡吃飯的時候，有很多稅吏和罪人來與他和門徒一起吃飯。

en: And when the Pharisees saw this, they said to his disciples, "Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?"
zh: 法利賽人看見了，就對他的門徒說：“你們的老師為甚麼跟稅吏和罪人一起吃飯呢？”

en: But when he heard it, he said, "Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
zh: 耶穌聽見了，就說：“健康的人不需要醫生，有病的人才需要，

en: Go and learn what this means, 'I desire mercy, and not sacrifice.' For I came not to call the righteous, but sinners."
zh: ‘我喜愛憐憫，不喜愛祭祀’，你們去想一想這話的意思吧。我來不是要召義人，而是要召罪人。”

en: Then the disciples of John came to him, saying, "Why do we and the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?"
zh: 那時，約翰的門徒前來問耶穌：“為甚麼我們和法利賽人常常禁食，你的門徒卻不禁食呢？”

en: And Jesus said to them, "Can the wedding guests mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? The days will come when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they will fast.
zh: 耶穌回答：“新郎跟賓客在一起的時候，賓客怎能哀痛呢？但到了時候，新郎就要從他們中間被取去，那時他們就要禁食了。

en: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch tears away from the garment, and a worse tear is made.
zh: 沒有人會拿一塊新布補在舊衣服上，因為補上的會把衣服扯破，裂的地方就更大了。

en: Neither is new wine put into old wineskins. If it is, the skins burst and the wine is spilled and the skins are destroyed. But new wine is put into fresh wineskins, and so both are preserved."
zh: 也沒有人會把新酒裝在舊皮袋裡，如果這樣，皮袋就會脹破，酒就漏出來，皮袋也損壞了。人總是把新酒裝在新皮袋裡，這樣，兩樣都可以保全。”

en: While he was saying these things to them, behold, a ruler came in and knelt before him, saying, "My daughter has just died, but come and lay your hand on her, and she will live."
zh: 耶穌對他們說話的時候，有一位會堂的主管走來跪在他面前，說：“我的女兒剛死了，但請你來按手在她身上，她必活過來。”

en: And Jesus rose and followed him, with his disciples.
zh: 於是耶穌和門徒起來跟著他去了。

en: And behold, a woman who had suffered from a discharge of blood for twelve years came up behind him and touched the fringe of his garment,
zh: 有一個女人，患了十二年的血漏病，她走到耶穌背後，摸他衣服的繸子，

en: for she said to herself, "If I only touch his garment, I will be made well."
zh: 因為她心裡說：“只要摸到他的衣服，我就必痊愈。”

en: Jesus turned, and seeing her he said, "Take heart, daughter; your faith has made you well." And instantly the woman was made well.
zh: 耶穌轉過來看見她，就說：“女兒，放心！你的信心使你痊愈了。”從那時起，那女人就好了。

en: And when Jesus came to the ruler's house and saw the flute players and the crowd making a commotion,
zh: 耶穌進了那主管的家，看見有吹笛的人和喧嘩的群眾，

en: he said, "Go away, for the girl is not dead but sleeping." And they laughed at him.
zh: 就說：“出去！這女孩不是死了，只是睡了。”他們就嘲笑他。

en: But when the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took her by the hand, and the girl arose.
zh: 耶穌趕走眾人之後，進去拉著女孩的手，女孩就起來了。

en: And the report of this went through all that district.
zh: 這消息傳遍了那一帶。

en: And as Jesus passed on from there, two blind men followed him, crying aloud, "Have mercy on us, Son of David."
zh: 耶穌離開那裡的時候，有兩個瞎子跟著他，喊著說：“大衛的子孫，可憐我們吧！”

en: When he entered the house, the blind men came to him, and Jesus said to them, "Do you believe that I am able to do this?" They said to him, "Yes, Lord."
zh: 耶穌進了房子，他們來到他那裡。耶穌問他們：“你們信我能作這些事嗎？”他們回答：“主啊，我們信。”

en: Then he touched their eyes, saying, "According to your faith be it done to you."
zh: 於是耶穌摸他們的眼睛，說：“照你們的信心給你們成就吧。”

en: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly warned them, "See that no one knows about it."
zh: 他們的眼睛就看見了。耶穌嚴嚴地囑咐他們：“千萬不可讓人知道。”

en: But they went away and spread his fame through all that district.
zh: 他們卻出去，把他所作的事傳遍了那一帶。

en: As they were going away, behold, a demon-oppressed man who was mute was brought to him.
zh: 他們出去的時候，有人帶著一個被鬼附著的啞巴來見耶穌。

en: And when the demon had been cast out, the mute man spoke. And the crowds marveled, saying, "Never was anything like this seen in Israel."
zh: 耶穌把鬼趕走之後，啞巴就說話了。眾人都很驚奇，說：“這樣的事，在以色列從來沒有見過。”

en: But the Pharisees said, "He casts out demons by the prince of demons."
zh: 但法利賽人說：“他不過是靠鬼王趕鬼罷了。”

en: And Jesus went throughout all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom and healing every disease and every affliction.
zh: 耶穌走遍各城各鄉，在各會堂裡教導人，宣揚天國的福音，醫治各種疾病、各種病症。

en: When he saw the crowds, he had compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
zh: 他看見群眾，就憐憫他們，因為他們困苦無依，像沒有牧人的羊一樣。

en: Then he said to his disciples, "The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few;
zh: 他就對門徒說：“莊稼多，工人少；

en: therefore pray earnestly to the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest."
zh: 所以你們應當求莊稼的主派工人去收割他的莊稼。”
